question_text: A student carries out an experiment using a light source, a diffraction grating, and a screen. She notices that the central bright spot is white, but the first-order diffraction spots show separated colours with red on the outside and violet on the inside. Which statement best explains this observation?

options.label: A
options.text: Red light has a longer wavelength than violet light, so it diffracts at a larger angle from the grating.

options.label: B
options.text: Red light travels faster through the diffraction grating than violet light, causing it to spread more.

options.label: C
options.text: Violet light is absorbed more by the grating, so red light diffracts more noticeably.

options.label: D
options.text: The diffraction grating is made of red and violet material, which naturally separates these colours.

explanation: Diffraction angle increases with wavelength. Red light has a longer wavelength than violet light, so it diffracts through a larger angle and appears further from the centre. This is why red appears on the outside of the diffraction pattern.
